title: Emma's Big Feelings Day
synopsis: Emma experiences big feelings when her favorite teddy bear goes missing, learning that it's okay to feel sad and scared while discovering how family love helps us feel brave and happy again.

pages.text: Emma loved her soft brown teddy bear named Cocoa more than anything in the whole world. Every morning, she would hug Cocoa tight and whisper, "Good morning, my best friend!" Cocoa had fuzzy ears, a pink bow, and the softest belly perfect for snuggling. Emma felt so happy when Cocoa was near.

illustration.description: A cheerful South Asian toddler girl with dark curly hair in two small ponytails sits on a bright yellow bed, hugging a brown teddy bear with a pink bow. Emma has big brown eyes and wears purple pajamas with star patterns. The bedroom has soft morning light streaming through curtains, with toys scattered around. The illustration uses warm, inviting colors and shows Emma's joyful expression as she cuddles her beloved teddy.
illustration.alt: Emma hugging her teddy bear Cocoa on her bed in the morning

pages.text: But this morning was different. Emma looked everywhere for Cocoa. She searched under her pillow, behind her toy box, and even in the laundry basket. "Where is Cocoa?" Emma asked, feeling worried. Her tummy felt funny, and her eyes began to fill with tears. Emma felt sad and scared because Cocoa was gone.

illustration.description: Emma kneeling on the floor of her colorful bedroom, looking under furniture with a concerned expression. Her dark hair is slightly messy from searching, and she wears a bright red t-shirt and blue overalls. The room shows signs of searching - pillows moved, toy box open, clothes scattered. The lighting is softer to match Emma's worried mood, with muted blues and purples in the background to convey her sad feelings.
illustration.alt: Emma searching her messy room looking worried and sad

pages.text: "Mama! Papa!" Emma called out, her voice shaky. Her parents, Mama Priya and Papa David, came running. Mama had long black hair and wore a cozy green sweater. Papa had gentle brown eyes and a kind smile. "Cocoa is missing!" Emma cried, and big tears rolled down her cheeks. "I feel so sad!" Mama and Papa wrapped Emma in a warm hug.

illustration.description: Emma standing in the hallway with tears on her cheeks, being embraced by her parents. Mama Priya has long black hair and South Asian features, wearing a soft green cardigan. Papa David has brown skin, short curly hair, and wears a blue button-up shirt. The family group hug takes place in a warm, well-lit hallway with family photos on the walls. The composition shows the comfort and security of family love, with soft golden lighting.
illustration.alt: Emma crying while being hugged by her diverse parents in the hallway

pages.text: "It's okay to feel sad when we lose something special," Mama said gently, stroking Emma's hair. "Your feelings are important, and we're here to help." Papa nodded and said, "Let's be brave detectives together! We'll search the whole house for Cocoa." Emma wiped her tears and felt a little spark of hope. Maybe being brave meant asking for help when you feel scared.

illustration.description: The family sitting together on a plush sofa in the living room. Mama Priya gently strokes Emma's hair while Papa David points toward the rest of the house with an encouraging expression. Emma sits between them, still teary but looking slightly more hopeful. The living room has warm earth tones, family photos, and colorful cushions. Soft natural light creates a comforting atmosphere, showing how family support helps during difficult feelings.
illustration.alt: Family sitting on couch planning to search for the missing teddy bear together

pages.text: They looked in every room together. Papa checked the kitchen cabinets while Emma looked behind the curtains. Mama searched the bathroom and the closets. Finally, they reached the living room couch where Grandma Rose was knitting. Grandma Rose had silver-white hair and wore purple glasses. "Are you looking for this little friend?" she asked with a twinkle in her eye.

illustration.description: The family searching throughout different rooms of the house, shown in a split composition. Emma peers behind bright yellow curtains, Papa opens white kitchen cabinets, and Mama looks in a closet. In the foreground, elderly Grandma Rose with silver hair, dark skin, and purple glasses sits in a cozy armchair with knitting needles and colorful yarn. The illustration shows movement and determination, with bright, optimistic colors suggesting the search is almost over.
illustration.alt: Family searching the house while Grandma Rose knits in her chair

pages.text: There was Cocoa, snuggled up next to Grandma Rose! "I found Cocoa on the floor this morning," Grandma explained. "I thought she might like to keep me company while I knit." Emma felt so happy and relieved! She hugged Cocoa tight and then gave Grandma Rose the biggest hug. "Thank you, Grandma!" Emma said. "I was scared, but now I feel brave and happy again!"

illustration.description: A joyful reunion scene showing Emma hugging both Cocoa the teddy bear and Grandma Rose. Emma's face radiates happiness and relief, with bright sparkling eyes and a big smile. Grandma Rose beams with joy, her purple glasses reflecting warm light. Cocoa the brown teddy bear with the pink bow is squeezed between them. The background is filled with warm golden light and soft rainbow colors to convey the happiness and love of the moment. Knitting supplies and colorful yarn add cozy details.
illustration.alt: Emma happily hugging both her found teddy bear and Grandma Rose

pages.text: That night, as Mama tucked Emma into bed, Emma hugged Cocoa close. "I learned something important today," Emma said sleepily. "It's okay to feel sad and scared sometimes. But when I'm brave enough to ask for help, my family is always there for me." Mama kissed Emma's forehead. "And that makes us all feel happy," Mama whispered. Emma smiled, feeling safe, loved, and very, very happy.

illustration.description: A peaceful bedtime scene with Emma tucked into her yellow bed, holding Cocoa the teddy bear. Mama Priya sits on the edge of the bed, leaning over to give Emma a goodnight kiss. The room is softly lit by a warm bedside lamp with star-shaped light patterns on the walls. Emma looks content and sleepy, with a gentle smile. The color palette uses soft purples, warm yellows, and gentle blues to create a calm, loving atmosphere perfect for sweet dreams.
illustration.alt: Mama tucking Emma and Cocoa into bed at nighttime

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Emma feel when she couldn't find Cocoa?
questions.explanation: Emma felt sad and scared when Cocoa was missing. It's normal to feel this way when we lose something important to us.

questions.question: Who found Cocoa the teddy bear?
questions.explanation: Grandma Rose found Cocoa on the floor and kept the teddy bear company while she was knitting.

questions.question: What did Emma learn about being brave?
questions.explanation: Emma learned that being brave can mean asking for help when you feel scared or sad.

questions.question: How did Emma's family help her feel better?
choices: They gave her hugs and helped search
They told her not to cry
They bought a new teddy bear
questions.explanation: Emma's family gave her hugs, told her it was okay to feel sad, and helped her search for Cocoa together.

pages.title: Feeling Words
pages.text: Emma felt ___ when Cocoa was missing. But when her family helped her search, she felt ___ enough to keep looking. When she found Cocoa, Emma felt very ___!

pages.title: Family Helpers
pages.text: ___ searched the kitchen cabinets. ___ looked in the closets. ___ was knitting and found Cocoa!
